issue_title: Stop social context from corrupting speaker names
issue_body:
**Phase:** Harden — making the existing flow actually work

Fix the social-context name handling so profile-derived spelling hints improve transcript and caption accuracy without altering already-correct host or guest names entered during episode setup. Speaker identities should remain the user's source of truth everywhere downstream, including setup summaries, audio polish, captions, review, templates, and export metadata.

## Acceptance (definition of done)
Given a setup with a speaker named "Sam Rivera" and social links that produce similar spelling hints, the running product keeps the speaker name exactly "Sam Rivera" through setup, contextual text suggestions, captions/transcript correction, review, saved templates, and export. Social context may still suggest spellings for topics, brands, references, and transcript terms, but it must not append or rewrite characters in confirmed speaker names such as changing "Sam Rivera" to "Sam Riveraa".

---
This is the current active step for this product. The maintainer will only merge a pull request that completes THIS step and demonstrably works. Open a PR that closes this issue.

_Why this step now: Checklist: Create episode by Riverside link or synced uploads: works. Assign files to Host / Guest 1 / Guest 2 buckets: works. Add social links so the product understands names, topics, references, brands, and spellings: partial because bug #41 can corrupt already-correct speaker names. Choose preset style, preview, and apply it: works. Canvas editor for reusable layouts: works, with an open layer-locking hardening bug still remaining. Audio cleanup and balancing controls: works. Contextual captions, b-roll, callouts, title moments, and engagement patterns: works. Save reusable show templates: partial because reapplying a saved identity has a reported speaker-retention bug. Export polished long-form episode: works, with review/export already built. Show library grouped by podcast identity and persisted IDs: works per completed fixes. The next step should target the highest-impact open core bug: social context must not damage speaker identity, because that undermines captions, templates, review, and export accuracy._
